meta.description: Satellite and GIS data source configurations for DSSM excavation zone prediction
meta.note: All APIs are free/open-source. API keys stored in .env file.

lidar_datasets.note: Point cloud LiDAR available for specific regions — US, parts of Europe, NZ, Australia
lidar_datasets.best_for: Settlement mound detection, earthwork mapping, buried site outlines

copernicus_cdse.name: Copernicus Data Space Ecosystem (CDSE) — Sentinel-1 + Sentinel-2
copernicus_cdse.auth: Free registration at dataspace.copernicus.eu — OAuth2 token
copernicus_cdse.note: Replaced SciHub in 2023. Free for all users after registration.

sentinel_1.penetration: Can penetrate dry soil 1–3m, vegetation canopy
best_for: Arid zone subsurface feature detection (Sahara, Middle East, Central Asia)
Crop mark detection over buried sites
Flood inundation mapping for site risk assessment
NDVI crop mark analysis over buried sites

key_indices.SAVI: Soil-adjusted vegetation index — better for arid zones

copernicus_cdse.search_endpoint_example: GET /odata/v1/Products?$filter=Collection/Name eq 'SENTINEL-2' and OData.CSC.Intersects(area=geography'SRID=4326;POINT({lon} {lat})') and ContentDate/Start gt {date}T00:00:00.000Z&$top=10

nasa_srtm.name: NASA SRTM — Shuttle Radar Topography Mission
nasa_srtm.auth: NASA Earthdata account (free registration) OR use via OpenTopography
nasa_srtm.best_for: Global baseline topography, site setting analysis
nasa_srtm.note: Best accessed via OpenTopography API as proxy — same data, simpler auth

open_context.name: Open Context — Archaeological Dataset Repository
open_context.auth: None — completely open, no key required
open_context.best_for: Known archaeological site data, excavation reports, artifact databases

pleiades.name: Pleiades — Ancient World Gazetteer
pleiades.best_for: Historical/ancient place names, coordinates for ancient sites

google_earth_engine.name: Google Earth Engine — Cloud Geospatial Analysis
google_earth_engine.auth: Free for research/academic — Google account + project approval
google_earth_engine.note: Requires Python or JS client. For server-side processing of large satellite datasets.
google_earth_engine.best_for: Time-series analysis, ML-based site prediction, large-area processing

bare_earth_model.description: Remove vegetation and buildings from LiDAR point cloud to reveal ground surface
bare_earth_model.method: Ground filtering algorithms (e.g., Progressive Morphological Filter)
bare_earth_model.reveals: Buried earthworks, mounds, ditches, field systems

hillshade.description: Simulate sunlight at low angles to reveal subtle topographic features
hillshade.reveals: Linear features, enclosures, trackways, building footprints

slope_analysis.description: Calculate rate of elevation change — sudden slope changes indicate artificial features

sky_view_factor.description: Proportion of visible sky from each point — flat-topped mounds show high SVF

local_relief_model.description: Remove regional topographic trend to isolate local features

javascript_libraries.raster_processing: geotiff.js — read GeoTIFF files in browser/Node
javascript_libraries.mapping: Leaflet.js — tile maps, GeoJSON overlay, marker clusters
javascript_libraries.server_lidar: PDAL (Point Data Abstraction Library) — Node.js bindings available

dssm_excavation_prediction_logic.description: How DSSM theory guides excavation zone prediction from satellite data

satellite_indicators: LiDAR: small circular mounds, pit alignments
LiDAR: platform mounds, enclosures, grid layouts
NDVI: crop marks over buried walls
LiDAR: grid street systems, large platforms
